reasoning: At 1x the apple reads as a vague warm blob — the round shape suggests food but apple identity is uncertain; no distinct stem/leaf silhouette visible at that scale. At 4x the pixel art is charming: warm red body, green leaf cluster, a creamy highlight patch give it friendly toy appeal. Contrast on dark bg is good (bright red pops). On the light bg the drop-shadow helps lift it but the highlight patch blends slightly into the cream background, softening perceived contrast. Edge cleanliness is fair — the pixel outline is well-defined at 4x but the highlight mass makes the top-right silhouette feel mushy rather than crisp. Personality is present (rounded, inviting colour palette, leaf tuft) but the highlight treatment trades silhouette clarity for a shiny effect, which is the main charm-vs-clarity tension here. Passes the sticker test on dark bg, marginal on light. Rounds to marginal overall.
